title: SACRAMENTO, CA
prompt_preview:

raw_text:
```json
{
  "claims": [
    "Governor Newsom has signed AB 2876 to integrate Artificial Intelligence (AI) literacy into the core curriculum for all K-12 students in California.",
    "AB 2876 responds to the rapid proliferation of AI in nearly every aspect of young people’s lives.",
    "AI has the potential to positively impact the way we live if we know how to use it responsibly.",
    "Children and young people today must navigate a world and job market transformed by fast-moving AI technology.",
    "All students must understand basic AI principles and applications, recognize when AI is employed, and be aware of AI’s implications, limitations, and ethical considerations.",
    "The law will equip California students with skills and training to be safe, ethical, and successful users of AI.",
    "Greater emphasis and resources are vital for preparing students with foundational knowledge and skills necessary to identify, understand, and successfully utilize AI.",
    "Digital literacy and AI literacy are basic skills that students need to develop for success in the modern world.",
    "Studies have shown that early exposure to technology concepts can significantly influence students’ career paths and preparedness for the future.",
    "The demand for AI-related professional skills is increasing across virtually every American industrial sector.",
    "AI systems are increasingly deployed in the real world, creating a disparity between those who develop AI and those who use it.",
    "Experts argue that AI literacy is something that every student needs exposure to, not just those planning on a career in computer science."
  ]
}
```
